name: D+Q reduced senescent-cell and SASP biomarkers in diabetic kidney disease
summary: The DKD pilot is a direct human biomarker anchor for Senolytics because short-course D+Q reduced adipose and skin senescence markers plus circulating SASP factors, but it remains preliminary.
statement: In nine diabetic kidney disease participants, three days of D+Q reduced adipose p16INK4A- and p21CIP1-expressing cells, senescence-associated beta-galactosidase activity, macrophage markers, skin p16INK4A and p21CIP1 cells, and circulating SASP factors including IL-1alpha, IL-6, MMP-9, and MMP-12.
population_or_model: Older adults with diabetic kidney disease
time_horizon: Biopsies and blood collected before treatment and 11 days after completing a three-day D+Q course
quantitative_note: The abstract reports N=9, mean age 68.7 years, and eGFR 27.0 mL/min/1.73m2.
caveats: Open-label phase 1 pilot with only nine participants.
Disease-specific tissue and inflammatory context may not generalize to healthy aging.
Biomarker movement does not prove durable functional, renal, morbidity, or mortality benefit.
Financial conflicts and senolytic patents were disclosed.
